document.title: User Manual for G1417/G1717 Series detector models

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual for H&abyz G1417/G1717 Series wireless digital flat panel detectors, including intended use, specifications, installation, maintenance, EMC, labeling, warranty, and revision history.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use this manual to set up the G1417/G1717 Series detector and understand its functions.
keyPoints: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors used for screening and diagnosis by qualified or trained doctors or technicians.
The detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.
The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
The estimated product lifetime may be up to five years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury.

concepts.name: Contra-indication
concepts.evidence: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

concepts.name: Electrical safety classification
concepts.evidence: Type of protection against electrical shock: Class l or Internal powered ME equipment Degree of protection against electrical shock: Class B applied part

concepts.name: Wireless communication
concepts.evidence: G1417/G1717 Series detectors support IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac to acquire images without a wired connection in anytime, anywhere.

concepts.name: EMC declaration
concepts.evidence: This equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.

concepts.name: Product disposal
concepts.evidence: When disposing of this product, therefore, be absolutely sure to follow the procedure that conforms to the laws and regulations applicable in your area.

concepts.name: Product lifetime
concepts.evidence: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five (5) years under the appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: The manual covers G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This user manual explains how to use the G1417/G1717 Series detector models made by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors for screening and diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series are digital flat panel detectors that is used for screening and diagnosis of disease of injury.

evidence.claim: The detectors are intended for use by qualified or trained doctors or technicians on adult subjects for diagnostic radiographic exposures.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are intended for use by a qualified/trained doctor or technician on both adult subjects for taking diagnostic radiographic exposures of the skull, spinal column, chest, abdomen, extremities, and other body parts.

evidence.claim: The G1417/G1717 Series patient group includes adults, children, and infants, with radiography reviewed by the doctor in charge before examination.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended for general patients such as adults, children, and infants, but all radiography should be reviewed by a doctor in charge prior to beginning the examination.

evidence.claim: The detectors are not intended for mammography applications.
evidence.evidenceText: These detectors are not intended to be used for mammography applications.

evidence.claim: The G1417/G1717 Series is identified as a medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a Medical Device.

evidence.claim: The detector scintillator provides CsI.
evidence.evidenceText: The scintillator provides CsI.

evidence.claim: The detectors support IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac wireless communication.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417/G1717 Series detectors support IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac to acquire images without a wired connection in anytime, anywhere.

evidence.claim: The G1417/G1717 Series supports AED function.
evidence.evidenceText: Supports the stable and reliable AED (Auto Exposure Detection) function.

evidence.claim: Electrical shock protection is Class I or internally powered ME equipment with Class B applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electrical shock: Class l or Internal powered ME equipment Degree of protection against electrical shock: Class B applied part

evidence.claim: Only a physician or legally certified operator should use the product.
evidence.evidenceText: Only a physician or a legally certified operator should use this product.

evidence.claim: Radiography, image processing, image reading, and image data storage must follow the laws of the country or region where the product is used.
evidence.evidenceText: Roentgenography, image processing, image reading, and image data storage must be performed in accordance with the laws of the country or region in which the product is being used.

evidence.claim: For EU and EEA disposal, the product is not to be disposed with household waste under WEEE Directive 2012/19/EC and national law.
evidence.evidenceText: This symbol indicates that this product is not to be disposed with your household waste, according to the WEEE Directive (2012/19/EC) and your national law.

evidence.claim: G1417/G1717 detector models include G1417CW, G1417CWP, G1417FW, G1417FWP, G1717CW, G1717CWP, G1717FW, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: Model Name G1417CW G1417CWP G1417FW G1417FWP G1717CW G1717CWP G1717FW G1717FWP

evidence.claim: The G1417 Series field of view is 14 x 17 inch and the G1717 Series field of view is 17 x 17 inch.
evidence.evidenceText: Field of View 14 x 17 inch 17 x 17 inch

evidence.claim: The detector IP range is IP68.
evidence.evidenceText: IP Range IP68

evidence.claim: The wireless communication specifications are the same across the listed G1417/G1717 Series models.
evidence.evidenceText: The wireless communication specifications of the G1417/G1717 Series detectors (G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, G1717FWP) are the same.

evidence.claim: The operating environment is 10 to 35 degrees C, 20 to 75 percent relative humidity, and 700 to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation Temperature 10~35℃ Humidity 20~75% H.R. Atmospheric pressure 700~1060hPa

evidence.claim: The load limit specifications are the same across the listed G1417/G1717 Series models.
evidence.evidenceText: The load limit specifications for G1417/G1717 Series detectors (G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, G1717FWP) are the same.

evidence.claim: The MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ battery pack is Li-Polymer, DC 11.55 V, 3330 mAh, and 3S1P.
evidence.evidenceText: Model MBP-3S1P3330-HAZ Type Li-Polymer Nominal Voltage DC 11.55V Nominal Capacity 3,330mAh Number of Cells 3S1P (3 Series 1 Parallel)

evidence.claim: The detector can be connected by wired connection, Soft AP connection, or wireless connection through a separate router.
evidence.evidenceText: You can organize wired or wireless connection between the G1417/G1717 Series detector and Router. You can also choose other connection ways suitable for the use environment in case of need

evidence.claim: The G1417/G1717 Series supports hot swap battery functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: G1417 / G1717 series detectors support ‘Hot Swap’ functionality.

evidence.claim: The detector should be cleaned with 70 percent IPA when contaminated.
evidence.evidenceText: Clean the detector with 70% IPA (Isopropyl-alcohol) when it is contaminated.

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five years with appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime may be up to five (5) years under the appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: The equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment complies with IEC 60601-1-2 EMC standards for medical devices.

evidence.claim: The wireless detector RF emissions classification is CISPR 11 Group 1 Class A.
evidence.evidenceText: RF Emissions CISPR 11 Group 1 RF Emissions CISPR 11 Class A

evidence.claim: The revision history lists version 0.0 dated 2024-07-22 as the initial release.
evidence.evidenceText: Version Date Descriptions 0.0 2024-07-22 Initial release
evidence.pageTarget: G1417/G1717 Series User Manual Revision History

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is MFDS/domestic in source metadata, but the document text does not explicitly name MFDS; authorityOrJurisdiction also includes jurisdictions mentioned in wireless and disposal sections.
The document title is inferred from the manual description and source file name because no standalone title page text is present in the extracted source.
Some OCR or extraction artifacts are present, including apparent typos such as '820.11ac', 'Csl', and inconsistent battery accessory model text 'MBP-3S1P33330-HAZ'.
